title: Day 8 · Interactive Shell + Plan Mode
subtitle: This map keeps the four Day 8 lines that matter: splitting input from the Agent, turn lifecycle, parallel read-only tools, and the Plan Mode approval loop.

diagrams.label: Runtime split
diagrams.title: Why the prompt stays on the main thread
diagrams.summary: PromptSession owns terminal input on the main thread; the worker thread runs run_agent; normal trace output goes through patch_stdout, while confirmation UI borrows the terminal with run_in_terminal.

nodes.label: CLI Runtime
nodes.detail: The REPL branch no longer starts a typer.prompt input thread. It creates RuntimeState, ToolRegistry, then enters run_interactive_shell.

nodes.label: PromptSession
nodes.detail: The main thread owns prompt_toolkit input, ESC / shift+tab bindings, and bottom_toolbar. Agent output no longer competes for stdin.

nodes.label: Slash dispatch
nodes.detail: Slash commands are the harness control plane. The main thread dispatches them directly instead of sending /model or /plan to the model.

nodes.label: job_queue
nodes.detail: Normal user prompts go into job_queue and are consumed serially by the worker thread. While busy, v3 redirects new input into RuntimeState.input_queue.

nodes.label: worker thread
nodes.detail: The worker thread blocks on provider.complete and tool execution. Each turn clears abort_event at the start so a previous ESC does not leak.

nodes.label: run_agent
nodes.detail: run_agent now receives RuntimeState instead of a fixed startup permission_mode. Permission, todo, and plan state all read from one runtime object.

nodes.label: patch_stdout
nodes.detail: console.print from the worker is intercepted by patch_stdout and safely rendered above the input line without corrupting the prompt.

nodes.label: run_in_terminal
nodes.detail: Confirmation UI cannot read stdin directly from the worker. prompt_ui._ask schedules the question back onto the main event loop, and run_in_terminal pauses the prompt first.

diagrams.label: Turn lifecycle
diagrams.title: How queued input and ESC settle
diagrams.summary: busy Event sends input typed during a run into RuntimeState.input_queue; ESC only sets abort_event; Agent Loop checks it between steps; Stop hook can force up to two continuation turns.

nodes.label: user input
nodes.detail: The user can type at any time. The key question is whether the worker is already running a turn.

nodes.label: busy Event
nodes.detail: The worker calls busy.set() before a turn and busy.clear() in finally. The main thread uses it to decide whether to submit or queue new input.

nodes.label: RuntimeState.input_queue
nodes.detail: Input typed during a run goes into the type-ahead queue. When the current turn ends, the worker drains it back into job_queue.

nodes.label: run_agent step
nodes.detail: provider.complete is blocking. Day 8 does not implement token-level cancellation; it can only stop after the current model/tool step returns.

nodes.label: abort_event
nodes.detail: The ESC handler only sets a flag. run_agent checks it after appending the assistant message and before executing tools.

nodes.label: is_error tool_result
nodes.detail: If the model already emitted tool_use but the user pressed ESC, the harness still returns one is_error tool_result per tool_use to keep Anthropic messages paired.

nodes.label: Stop hook
nodes.detail: When the model is about to end with final text, run_hooks_raw('Stop') can push one more turn via non-zero exit plus output. Day 8 caps this at two continuations.

nodes.label: drain queue
nodes.detail: After a normal end, interrupt, or exhausted Stop hook, the worker drains the type-ahead queue and schedules the queued prompt.

diagrams.label: Parallel tools
diagrams.title: Read-only tools in parallel, writes serial
diagrams.summary: A model can return multiple tool_use blocks in one turn, but the harness decides parallelism from Tool.is_read_only. Contiguous read-only tools run in parallel; writes split the batches; tool_result order still matches tool_use order.

nodes.label: ModelResponse.tool_calls
nodes.detail: The model may request several tools in one response. That list order is the protocol order tool_result blocks must preserve.

nodes.label: Tool.is_read_only
nodes.detail: Day 8 adds is_read_only to Tool. read_file, grep, git_status and similar tools are True; file_write, file_edit, and bash remain False.

nodes.label: partition_tool_calls
nodes.detail: Contiguous read-only tools become one parallel batch. A write or unknown tool closes the current read batch and becomes its own serial batch. Unknown tools fail closed as serial.

nodes.label: ThreadPoolExecutor.map
nodes.detail: Read-only batches run in parallel. map returns results in input order, so tool_result blocks stay aligned with tool_use order even if tools finish out of order.

nodes.label: ReadFileState lock
nodes.detail: Parallel read_file calls can write ctx.read_state.entries at the same time, so ReadFileState.record() wraps writes in threading.Lock.

nodes.label: serial write batch
nodes.detail: Write tools can mutate the filesystem or external state, so they run serially. Reads after a write should see the new state, which is why writes split batches.

nodes.label: tool_result blocks
nodes.detail: No matter how execution is scheduled internally, the user message returned to the model contains tool_result blocks in the original tool_use order.

nodes.label: messages
nodes.detail: The assistant tool_use message and the following user tool_result message both enter context before the next complete call.

diagrams.label: Plan approval
diagrams.title: The only path that unlocks writes in Plan Mode
diagrams.summary: Plan mode allows reads, todo tools, and plan tools; all other write tools are denied. Whether the model calls exit_plan_mode or returns final plan text, confirm_plan must be approved before permission_mode flips to acceptEdits.

nodes.label: permission_mode=plan
nodes.detail: All three entry points modify RuntimeState.permission_mode. There is no second copy of mode state.

nodes.label: decide_permission
nodes.detail: Plan mode allows read-only tools, todo_read/todo_write, enter_plan_mode, and exit_plan_mode. file_write, file_edit, bash, and other write-like tools are denied.

nodes.label: draft plan
nodes.detail: The model can inspect code, update todos, and draft a plan, but it cannot modify files before approval.

nodes.label: exit_plan_mode or final text
nodes.detail: Some models call exit_plan_mode(plan_summary); others return the plan as final text. Day 8 routes both through the same turn-boundary approval gate.

nodes.label: confirm_plan
nodes.detail: confirm_plan pauses the prompt with run_in_terminal, renders the Plan panel first, then asks the user. This is the only place writes unlock.

nodes.label: same-turn writes skipped
nodes.detail: If the model submits a plan and sneaks file_write into the same turn, the harness only processes the approval gate. The other tool_use blocks receive error tool_result blocks and are not executed.

nodes.label: acceptEdits
nodes.detail: After approval, RuntimeState.permission_mode becomes acceptEdits. File writes no longer ask one by one, but bash still requires command confirmation.

nodes.label: write tools
nodes.detail: Only the next turn re-issues execution tools and actually mutates the filesystem.
